mnemonics.title: Forms of Energy Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: KETCRUP - Kinetic, Electrical, Thermal, Chemical, Radiant (Light), Universal (Nuclear), Potential. Remember: 'Kids Eat Toast Carefully Running Under Playground' = Kinetic, Electrical, Thermal, Chemical, Radiant, Nuclear, Potential.

mnemonics.title: Renewable Energy Sources
mnemonics.content: SHAVWG - Solar, Hydro, Agricultural (Biomass), Wind, Wave, Geothermal. Think: 'Sam Had A Very Weird Garden'.

mnemonics.title: Energy Efficiency Tips
mnemonics.content: SAVE - Smart use, Audit consumption, Verify ratings, Efficient appliances. Remember: 'Switch Always to LED or Efficient bulbs'.

acronyms.expansion: Green Renewable Energy for Environmental Exploitation
acronyms.usage: Refers to environmentally friendly renewable energy sources that minimize pollution and carbon footprint.
Framework for evaluating clean energy alternatives.
Concept for understanding primary vs secondary energy forms.

visualTips.visualization: Draw flowchart: Fuel (Chemical) → Boiler (Thermal) → Turbine (Mechanical) → Generator (Electrical) → Grid. Each arrow shows energy conversion with typical efficiency percentages.

visualTips.concept: Potential vs Kinetic Energy in Pendulum
visualTips.visualization: A pendulum: at top = 100% PE (max height), at bottom = 100% KE (max speed). Draw sine wave showing conversion. Energy bar chart showing inverse relationship.
Pyramid: Base = Primary (fossil fuels, uranium), Middle = Secondary (electricity, hydrogen), Top = Useful (light, motion, heat). Shows energy quality decreasing as we move up.

analogies.analogy: Energy is like money - it can be stored (potential), exchanged (work), and transferred between 'accounts' (systems). Just as money flows through economy, energy flows through ecosystems.
Kinetic energy is like a running athlete - the faster they run (higher velocity) or heavier they are (more mass), the harder they can hit (more energy).
Potential energy is like water stored in a dam - it's waiting to be released. The higher the dam (more height), the more potential energy stored, ready to do work when released.
Conservation of energy is like a fixed amount of water in a closed system - it can change form (ice, water, vapor) but total amount stays same. You can't create or destroy water, only transform it.
